prompt:
A thin spring-steel print removal scraper with a wide, flat blade and an ergonomic handle is shown sliding under the edge of a 3D print on a build plate. The blade is flexible and slightly beveled, clearly thin enough to lift parts without gouging the surface, and the handle is shaped for a comfortable, secure grip with no branding or text. A partially lifted rectangular print demonstrates how the tool is used to gently pop prints free.

The scene is a near-future solarpunk maker space with a 3D printer occupying the background, soft overhead lighting, and neatly organized tools nearby. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or legible markings on the scraper or printer, and a composition that focuses on the blade slipping under the print edge while the rest of the printer and workspace fades into a soft blur.
